Gate Delay Optimization

Source: R/preprocess.R
ls_gate_optimize.Rd

Analyzes SNR and signal-to-background ratios across spectra acquired at different gate delays to recommend optimal timing for a target line.

Usage

ls_gate_optimize(spectra, element, line_nm, window_nm = 1)

Arguments

spectra

List of ls_spectrum() objects. Each spectrum's metadata should contain gate_delay_us.

element

Character. Target element symbol.

line_nm

Numeric. Target emission line wavelength (nm).

window_nm

Numeric. Window around the line. Default 1.

Value

A tibble::tibble() with columns gate_delay_us, snr, sbr, peak_intensity, with attribute "recommended" giving the row index of the highest SNR.

Examples

specs <- lapply(c(0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10), function(g) {
  s <- ls_simulate_spectrum(seed = round(g * 10))
  s$metadata$gate_delay_us <- g
  s
})
ls_gate_optimize(specs, "Ca", 393.37)
#> # A tibble: 5 × 4
#>   gate_delay_us   snr   sbr peak_intensity
#>           <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>          <dbl>
#> 1           0.5 10.6   1.10           77.1
#> 2           1    8.09  1.09           82.3
#> 3           2    9.48  1.09           81.0
#> 4           5   10.6   1.09           81.5
#> 5          10   10.8   1.10           78.0
